overview: Mono-Black Midrange in Modern leverages efficient disruption and card advantage to stabilize early and outvalue opponents in the late game. It relies on disruptive one-mana spells to dictate the board state before deploying resilient threats. The deck aims to control the pace without overcommitting resources, transitioning smoothly into a dominant endgame.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through superior card advantage and removal efficiency
Deploying Kozilek's Command as a game-ending threat or board wipe
Establishing resilient creatures like Thought-Knot Seer to apply consistent pressure

keep_criteria: Hands with at least one one-mana disruption spell
Hands containing a creature threat and a removal option
Two-mana curve hands that establish early board presence
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking disruption or early interaction
Mana-heavy hands with no one or two-drop
Hands that rely solely on late-game threats without setup

early_game: Play Thoughtseize on turn one to disrupt opponent's combo or aggressive strategy
Deploy Thought-Knot Seer to gain card advantage and apply early pressure
Hold disruption for high-value targets while establishing a minimal board presence
mid_game: Trade efficiently using removal to clear opposing threats
Use Kozilek's Command to stabilize or take the lead when the board is favorable
Maintain card parity by leveraging Seer's death trigger and cheap spells
late_game: Deploy Kozilek's Command as a primary win condition or board reset
Outvalue opponents by consistently generating card advantage
Close out games with resilient threats that are difficult to remove efficiently

key_cards.name: Thoughtseize
key_cards.notes: Use on turn one to remove key combo pieces, early threats, or game-winning spells. Prioritize targets that significantly alter the opponent's game plan.

key_cards.name: Thought-Knot Seer
key_cards.notes: Play early to trigger its death ability, drawing a card when it dies. Use it to block efficiently and generate value over multiple turns.

key_cards.name: Kozilek's Command
key_cards.notes: Deploy when you have a favorable board state or need to clear opposing creatures. Use its modal abilities to stabilize, draw cards, or finish the game.

sequencing_tips: Always play Thoughtseize before committing creatures to the board
Hold Kozilek's Command until you have a clear board advantage or need a reset
Trigger Thought-Knot Seer's death ability as early as possible to maximize card draw
Prioritize removing high-impact threats over low-value blockers

matchups.advice: Disrupt early game plans with Thoughtseize and establish resilient blockers. Use Kozilek's Command to clear boards and stabilize.
Apply early pressure with Seer and cheap threats. Use Kozilek's Command to outvalue their removal suite and close the game.
Identify and remove key combo pieces immediately with Thoughtseize. Disrupt their setup phase to prevent them from assembling their win condition.
